methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Whisper Aero is a Series A stage company in Space & Aerospace founded by Mark Moore and Ian Villa, having raised $40M+ to date. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs, though a narrow same-stage peer set proved too limited for direct median benchmarking.

Given data constraints, stage-progression anchoring is the applicable method. Using total raised ($40M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical Series A-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.0x yields an implied valuation of $80M. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures, which private companies typically withhold. A narrow peer-median approach was deemed unreliable given insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting quantitative thresholds within the Space & Aerospace vertical.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $68M to $92M, with $80M as the median point. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression methodology. Key uncertainties include (1) absence of revenue or unit economics data to anchor public multiples; (2) limited granular comparables at Series A stage in this sector; and (3) reliance on forward-looking stage multiples rather than trading multiples or precedent transactions. An expanded dataset on peer financials or disclosed revenue would strengthen conviction materially.
